overview: This appears to be a Tortoise Bont Tick (Amblyomma marmoreum), a species of hardbacked tick often found on tortoises. The specimen exhibits a distinctive ornate, white or light-colored pattern on its shield, contrasted with a dark reddish-brown body. These ticks are generally known to feed on reptiles and small mammals, acting as bloodsucking external parasites.
